title: Selling Presets Without Stripe: Which Option Actually Works for You — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: You’re in Nigeria, Pakistan, or Ghana, and you’ve just been blocked from selling your presets on Gumroad because Stripe doesn’t support your country. PayPal isn’t an option either—it’s either unavailable or holds funds indefinitely. You’ve got a digital product ready to sell, but the platforms you thought were global actually exclude you. This isn’t just frustrating; it’s a structural barrier that keeps creators in restricted countries from earning. The real problem? These platforms rely on payment processors that blacklist entire regions due to compliance risks, banking instability, or arbitrary policies. If you’re tired of waiting for permission, this post breaks down your actual options—not theoretical workarounds—to sell presets without Stripe or PayPal. We’ll compare what works, what doesn’t, and why crypto-native infrastructure is the permanent solution you’ve been looking for.
What Each Option Actually Offers

Stripe and PayPal dominate digital sales because they’re seamless—for those they allow. Stripe offers instant payouts, subscription models, and fraud protection, but it’s unavailable in countries like Nigeria, Ghana, and Pakistan due to regulatory hurdles. PayPal supports more regions but freezes accounts in high-risk zones, leaving creators stranded. Alternatives like Gumroad and Payhip are built on these processors, so they inherit the same restrictions. Crypto-native solutions, on the other hand, bypass this entirely. They don’t rely on banks or processors; instead, they use blockchain to accept payments globally. The tradeoff? You’re responsible for managing volatility and educating buyers on crypto payments. But if you’re excluded from the traditional system, this isn’t a tradeoff—it’s your only path forward.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

Stripe and PayPal exclude countries based on risk assessments, not potential. If your country has banking instability, high fraud rates, or regulatory gray areas, you’re out. Even if you use a VPN or intermediary, these platforms flag and suspend accounts tied to restricted regions. Gumroad and Payhip can’t help because they’re built on Stripe’s infrastructure. Crypto-native solutions flip this model. They don’t care where you’re from—they care that you have a wallet. With tools like Web3 Wallet Connect, buyers from any country can pay in BTC, ETH, or USDT, and you receive funds directly. There’s no middleman to block you, no KYC to fail, and no geographic restrictions. The system is permissionless by design, making it the only option for creators in blocked regions.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

Stripe charges 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction, but that’s irrelevant if you can’t use it. PayPal’s fees are similar, but they hold funds for 21 days in high-risk countries—if they don’t freeze your account first. Crypto transactions cost 1-3% depending on the network, but funds settle in minutes, not days. The real cost of traditional platforms? Lost sales. If you’re in a restricted country, you’re not just paying fees—you’re paying to be excluded. Crypto-native solutions have no monthly fees, no hidden costs, and no subscriptions. You pay once for the infrastructure and own it forever. The only catch? You need to manage price volatility, but stablecoins like USDT eliminate that risk entirely.
PayPal's geographic exclusion is not a bug — it is a deliberate infrastructure decision that will not change.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

Stripe and PayPal automate payments, but without access, their automation is useless. Gumroad and Payhip automate delivery, but their payment gates are locked for you. Crypto-native solutions automate both. Once payment is verified on the blockchain, your product is delivered instantly—no manual intervention required. Tools like Unchained Commerce’s Core Shop Engine handle this for you, generating PDF receipts and verifying payments without needing a processor account. The system is fully automated, global, and doesn’t require buyers to have existing accounts. It’s not just an alternative; it’s a better system for creators who’ve been shut out of the old one.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

If you’re in a Stripe or PayPal-supported country, stick with them—they’re simpler. But if you’re in Nigeria, Ghana, Pakistan, or any restricted region, crypto-native infrastructure is your only reliable option. It’s not just a workaround; it’s a structural solution that removes the middlemen blocking you. Yes, there’s a learning curve, but the alternative is staying locked out of the global market. Start with a tool like Web3 Wallet Connect to accept payments in 300+ coins, then pair it with an automated delivery system. The first sale you make will prove it’s worth it. Stop waiting for platforms to include you—build your own system and take control.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

The direct answer is NOWPayments combined with the Core Shop Engine. NOWPayments is a crypto payment processor that requires no KYC from merchants and no account creation from buyers. It supports over 300 coins and sends a webhook to your backend when a payment confirms on-chain. The Core Shop Engine receives that webhook, verifies the transaction, generates a receipt, and delivers the product automatically. The key difference between this and a platform workaround is that it is not a workaround — it is a different infrastructure layer entirely. There is no platform that can suspend your access, no compliance team that can flag your account, and no payout schedule that delays your revenue. Your funds go directly to a wallet you control the moment a transaction confirms.
Crypto-native infrastructure means no single platform decision can cut off your income.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

Creators researching PayPal alternatives often discover that the real problem is not finding a single substitute — it is rebuilding the entire payment and delivery stack on infrastructure that does not inherit conventional payment system restrictions. The Core Shop Engine at $49.99 handles the backend piece: payment verification, automated delivery, and receipt generation. Web3 Wallet Connect at $19.99 handles the buyer-facing checkout. Together, they replace what Gumroad or Payhip was doing, but without any platform that can restrict your access. The Full Stack Bundle at $79.99 includes both components plus the Admin Dashboard and deployment guide. Visit the shop at https://unchained-commerce.github.io/Shop/index.html to see the complete component breakdown.
Your Action Checklist

Start by accepting that no PayPal or Stripe workaround is durable — the platforms actively close gaps as they discover them. Create a NOWPayments account and configure wallet addresses for USDT on Tron and Bitcoin as your primary coins. Purchase and deploy the Core Shop Engine — it handles payment verification and automated delivery. Configure the Web3 Wallet Connect module with your product details and checkout settings. Upload your digital product files to your delivery server with the paths configured in the engine. Test the complete flow with a real mainnet transaction — send a small amount to your wallet and confirm the webhook fires and the delivery email arrives. Check the Admin Dashboard to confirm the transaction appears in the log. Add your store URL to your social profiles and content channels. You now have infrastructure that no payment platform can take from you.
The store that runs on a wallet and a webhook cannot be suspended by anyone else's compliance team.
Web3 Wallet Connect — $19.99. Frontend wallet integration with multi-chain QR generation and live price feeds. Let buyers pay in BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, and 300 more coins.
